objectives.descriptor_en: Recognizes greeting phrases and names when heard, and matches them.
Matches written greeting phrases to their meaning.
Greets and introduces themselves (name and age) using memorized phrases.
Writes their name, age, and a simple greeting (words / a short sentence).
Understands numbers from 1 to 20 when heard, and identifies them.
Reads numbers (words and numerals) from 1 to 20, and matches them.
Asks about name and age and answers (at least one back-and-forth question and answer).
Understands simple spoken classroom commands and matches them to the action / picture.
Matches words for classroom objects and colors to their pictures.
Names their classroom objects and their colors (this is a…, it's red).
Writes the names of classroom objects and colors (single words).
Recognizes the names of classroom objects and colors when heard, and matches them.
Asks "What is this?" and answers (at least one back-and-forth question and answer).
Understands someone naming their family members, and matches them to pictures.
Reads a very short text about a family and matches the members.
Introduces their family members in simple sentences (This is my…, He is my…).
Writes simple sentences about their family (This is my…).
Distinguishes simple family relationships when listening.
Matches family members to their roles based on a simple text.
Asks and answers about the family (at least one back-and-forth question and answer).
Understands a simple description of a person's appearance and matches it to a picture.
Matches a simple written description to the correct person's picture.
Describes their appearance in simple sentences (I have got…).
Writes simple sentences describing their appearance (I have got…).
Recognizes the names of body parts when heard.
Asks and answers about appearance in a simple way.
Understands a simple description of an animal (what it is and what it does) and matches it.
Reads a very short text about an animal and locates one simple piece of information.
Talks about their favorite animal and what it can do in simple sentences.
Writes one or two sentences about an animal (A cat can…).
Distinguishes what an animal can do when listening (can fly / swim).
Matches animal names to their pictures or to what they do in a simple text.
Asks and answers about animals and their abilities (at least one back-and-forth question and answer).
Understands someone stating which foods they like and dislike, and matches it.
Reads a simple list or a short text about food and matches it.
Talks about which foods they like and dislike (I like…, I don't like…).
Writes simple sentences about their favorite food (I like…).
Recognizes the names of foods and drinks when heard.
Asks and answers about food preferences (at least one back-and-forth question and answer).
Understands a simple description of a room and identifies where the things are.
Reads a simple description of a room and matches the things to their places.
Describes their room, their things, and their places in simple sentences (There is…, It's on…).
Writes simple sentences about the things in their room (There is a…).
Recognizes the names of room objects when heard.
Matches object names to their pictures based on a simple text.
Asks and answers about the location of things in a simple way (Where is…?).
Understands someone stating which activities they can do, and matches it.
Reads a very short text about a child's activities and locates what they can do.
Talks about what they can and cannot do (I can…, I can't…).
Writes simple sentences about what they can do (I can…).
Recognizes the names of activities and sports when heard.
Asks and answers about abilities and activities (at least one back-and-forth question and answer, Can you…?).
Understands a simple spoken weather report and matches the weather to the picture.
Reads a table of days or a simple report and locates the information.
Talks about today's weather and what they wear in simple sentences (It's sunny. I wear…).
Writes simple sentences about their days or today's weather.
Recognizes the days of the week and weather conditions when heard.
Matches weather conditions or clothes to their pictures in a simple text.
Asks and answers about the day and the weather (at least one back-and-forth question and answer).
Asks and tells the time simply (o'clock).
Understands someone introducing themselves and their family in a short recording, and identifies the names, ages, and relationships.
Reads an ID card or a short text about a person and locates their information.
Introduces themselves and their family in several connected sentences (name, age, country, relatives).
Writes a short introduction (5–6 sentences) about themselves or a family member.
Distinguishes the description of a person's appearance or personality when listening, and matches it to a picture.
Matches descriptions of people to their pictures based on a simple text.
Asks and answers about personal information in a short dialogue.
Understands someone describing their routine in a short, slow recording, and identifies the activities and their times.
Reads a short text about another child's day and locates its key information.
Describes their daily routine in 4–6 connected sentences with sufficient accuracy.
Writes a paragraph (5–6 sentences) about their weekly routine, using adverbs of frequency accurately.
Distinguishes how often an activity is done when listening (always / sometimes / never).
Sequences the events of a day described in a simple text correctly.
Asks and answers their classmate about hobbies and free time (at least one back-and-forth question and answer).
Writes short, correct questions to ask a friend about their hobbies.
Understands a description of a house or room and identifies what is in it and where.
Reads a text describing a neighborhood or city and locates the places and their locations.
Describes their house or room (what is in it and where things are) in several sentences.
Writes a short description (5–6 sentences) of their house or neighborhood, using there is/are and prepositions of place.
Follows simple spoken directions to reach a place on a simplified map.
Matches written directions to the correct route on a map.
Asks about a place and gives simple directions in a dialogue.
Understands an ordering or buying conversation and identifies what was ordered and its price.
Reads a menu or a price advertisement and locates the required information.
Orders food or buys something and asks about the price in a service dialogue (restaurant / shop).
Writes a shopping list or a description of their favorite meal (5–6 sentences).
Understands someone talking about how they feel health-wise and matches the symptom to the advice.
Reads a short text about healthy habits and identifies true and false.
Expresses their food preferences and asks about others' preferences.
Understands a spoken description of what people are doing now and matches it to the picture.
Reads a text describing an ongoing scene and locates the activities.
Describes a picture or a scene (who is in it and what they are doing and wearing) in several sentences.
Writes a description (5–6 sentences) of a picture, using the present continuous.
Distinguishes between a habit (present simple) and an action happening now (continuous) when listening.
Asks and answers about what people are doing now in a dialogue.
Understands someone recounting what they did in the past and identifies the main events and their order.
Reads a short story or diary in the past and locates its events.
Narrates what they did on a previous holiday or trip in several sequential sentences.
Writes a paragraph (6–8 sentences) about a past day or trip, using the past simple and sequence connectors.
Distinguishes past-tense verbs when listening and matches the event to its time.
Sequences the scrambled events of a story by relying on the sequence connectors.
Asks and answers about past events in a dialogue (What did you do? Where did you go?).
Writes a short message (3–4 sentences) to a friend telling them about something they did recently.
Understands a spoken comparison between two things / places and identifies the bigger / better one.
Reads a text comparing animals or places and locates the comparative information.
Compares two things or places, using comparative forms, in several sentences.
Writes a paragraph (5–6 sentences) comparing two things, using comparatives/superlatives.
Identifies comparative information (the tallest, the fastest) about animals or places when listening.
Locates the "most / biggest" information from a table or a simple text.
Expresses their opinion on the best / biggest and justifies it simply in a dialogue.
Understands someone talking about their upcoming plans and identifies what and when.
Reads a message or an advertisement about an upcoming event and locates its details.
Talks about their plans for an upcoming holiday or weekend in several sentences.
Writes a short message (5–6 sentences) telling a friend about their upcoming plans.
Matches people to their future plans when heard.
Agrees with their classmate on a shared plan (when / where / what) in a dialogue.
Invites someone to an activity and accepts or politely declines an invitation.
Understands someone talking about what they can / cannot do and matches it.
Reads a list of rules or instructions and distinguishes what is required from what is forbidden.
Talks about their abilities and skills (what they are good at and not) in several sentences.
Writes a short list of rules or advice (5–6 sentences), using must/should.
Understands the spoken rules of a place and distinguishes what is allowed from what is forbidden.
Reads a text about a person's / animal's abilities and locates the information.
Gives advice or states a rule to their classmate in a short dialogue.
Apologizes for a mistake and politely offers an excuse.
Understands an announcement or conversation about transport schedules and identifies the time and destination.
Reads a timetable or a ticket and locates the information (time, platform, price).
Asks about a route or transport time and handles a ticket-counter interaction (functional dialogue).
Writes a short postcard (5–6 sentences) about a trip (past) or a travel plan (future).
Understands someone describing a trip (how they traveled and where) and identifies the means of transport.
Reads a short text about a trip and locates the means of transport and places.
Narrates a trip they took (how and where) or plans an upcoming trip in several sentences.
Starts a short phone call and ends it politely.
Understands the chronological sequence of events in a short spoken story (what happened first / next).
Follows the sequence of events in a short written story and sequences them.
Narrates a past experience in chronological order, using the past simple and at least two connectors.
Writes a short paragraph (4–5 sentences) narrating a past experience ordered with sequence connectors.
Identifies specific details from a narration of a personal experience (when, where, who).
Distinguishes between what happened once and what was habitual (used to) in a narrative text.
Describes what they were doing at a particular past time, using the past continuous.
Understands a person's future plans from a spoken talk (what they will do, when).
Understands a short text about a person's plans and locates their main goal.
Talks about their future plans and ambitions, giving at least one reason.
Writes a short paragraph about a future ambition of theirs, with a reason and a step to achieve it.
Distinguishes between a definite plan and a possibility (might) in a recording.
Links ambitions to their reasons in a text.
Asks and answers about future intentions (What will you do in…?).
Understands a speaker's opinion and their reason in an audio clip.
Identifies the writer's opinion and stance in a short text.
Expresses their opinion on a familiar topic, giving a reason (because…).
Writes a paragraph expressing their opinion with two reasons linked by connectors.
Distinguishes between agreement and objection in a dialogue.
Agrees or politely objects to an opinion in a short discussion.
Writes a short text presenting their opinion and acknowledging an opposing one politely (although/however).
Leads a short discussion, inviting others to give their opinions and taking turns.
Understands a spoken description of a place and identifies its features.
Understands a short tourist text about a city and locates key information.
Describes a place they know, using a relative clause and a comparison.
Writes a short description of a place they visited or love, using comparisons and a relative clause.
Follows directions to reach a place.
Matches the description to the appropriate place in a comparative text.
Compares two places and justifies their preference.
Understands spoken health advice and identifies the recommendation.
Understands a text about healthy habits and locates the advice.
Gives health advice, using should/shouldn't.
Writes a paragraph of advice for staying healthy, using modal verbs.
Identifies symptoms and advice from a dialogue between a patient and a doctor.
Distinguishes between what must and what should not be done in an instructional text.
Describes a healthy habit and justifies its importance.
Understands a talk about using technology and identifies the speaker's experiences.
Understands a text about digital safety and locates the guidelines.
Asks and answers about experiences (Have you ever…?).
Writes a paragraph about a device they use: what they have done with it and why it is useful.
Distinguishes between a past experience (present perfect) and a specific past event in a recording.
Identifies the correct information from a text about a device or app.
Talks about the benefits and risks of technology, giving an example.
Writes an appropriate short post or comment online.
Communicates in a text chat to coordinate an activity with digital etiquette.
Understands suggestions for helping in an audio clip.
Understands a text about a community initiative and locates its goal.
Suggests a way to help a person or community, using a suggestion form.
Writes a short paragraph suggesting a project to help their neighbors / school, with an expected outcome (conditional).
Identifies a condition and a result from a spoken conditional sentence.
Talks about volunteer or charity work and why it is important.
Writes a short advertisement / poster inviting their classmates to take part in volunteer work.
Understands a talk about school subjects and a speaker's preferences.
Understands a text about study skills and locates the advice.
Talks about the subjects they like / prefer and why, using verb patterns.
Writes a paragraph about their favorite subject and study habits.
Identifies study tips from an audio clip.
Links each subject to its description in a text.
Compares two learning methods and expresses their preference (would rather/prefer).
Understands a spoken explanation of a simple natural phenomenon (cause and effect).
Understands a short informational text about the environment and locates the main idea.
Explains a cause and effect in nature, using the conditional (if/when…).
Writes a paragraph about an environmental problem and a proposed solution for it.
Identifies environmental steps (such as recycling) from a clip.
Understands a process described in the simple passive in a text.
Suggests habits for protecting the environment.
Understands a description of a person's feelings and their cause in a clip.
Understands a text about a friendship or family relationship and locates the values / feelings.
Describes their feelings in a situation and justifies them.
Writes a paragraph about a friend / family member: their qualities and why they value them.
Distinguishes between the feeling (-ed) and the cause (-ing) in a recording.
Identifies the person described by a relative clause (who…) in a text.
Reports what another person said in a simple form (he said that…).
Expresses sympathy for someone in an appropriate situation.
Understands a spoken description of an occasion and identifies its details (when, how it is celebrated).
Understands a text about an occasion / tradition and locates its main elements.
Describes an occasion important to them (such as Eid): what they do and how they feel.
Writes a paragraph describing an occasion they love and why it is special to them.
Compares two traditions based on an audio clip.
Reads two short texts about two traditions and identifies a similarity and a difference.
Compares their tradition with another politely and respectfully.
Writes a short greeting / invitation card appropriate to the event.

titles.A2-U02: My Day and My Free Time
